title: The Brew Shop - Weekly Email Update
contentHtml:
Hey Brew Shop Fam!
Here's an update of what's happening this week - from new beer drops, wine reviews, our current tap list, to upcoming tastings, and more. 

We will have special holiday hours on Saturday, July 4th! Please note, we will not have a tasting this Friday, July 3, but scroll down to see our full tasting line-up for July.

Fresh drop from shop fav Schilling

• Aosta Italian Pilsner

• Maly 8 Czech Session Pilsner

• Steinach Dark German Lager

• Ponyhawk DIPA with Simcoe, Mosaic & Galaxy

Estate brews from Wheatland Spring

• Paddock Estate Pale Lager

• Liberty Estate Wheat Ale

• Meridian Extra Pale Ale

The gang from TN dropping a World Beer Cup medal winner

• Xul Lost to History DIPA with Citra, Motueka & Nelson

Get your fruit servings with Drekker

• Braaaaains Billions of Peaches Sour with peaches & apricots

• Chonk Merica Cake Sour with strawberries, blueberries, banana, cake batter, vanilla beans, & lactose

• Fluff Stuff Cherry Sour with cherry & marshmallow

• Juiceler DIPA with Citra, Mosaic & Sabro

🐶 The latest limited releases from RaR!
• Just Squeezed DIPA with Citra, Lotus & Nectaron
• Double Pulp DIPA with Citra & Motueka
• Slip Ons IPA with Nelson, Motueka, Rakau & Waimea
• Out Of Order: Heads Will Roll Lactose Sour with Triple Strawberry, House Pastry Cream & Cookie Dough

😍 Always happy to see some bangers from out of town
• Zero Gravity Pisolino Italian Pilsner
• Parish Dr Juice Grapefruit IPA with Cascade & Citra plus Grapefruit

🦖 Rawrrrrr Toppling Goliath
• DDH King Sue DDH DIPA with Citra
• Sue Mixed Pack

🏄 Commonwealth fresh in from VA Beach
• Maui’s Hook IPA with Lilikoi & Citra
• Southern Energy DIPA with Peacharine & Nectaron
• Shaolin Temple Gose with Kiwi, Dragon Fruit, White Grape & Green Tea

🌳 Benchtop keeping Julie happy
• Wishing Tree Unfiltered German Pilsner
• Soft Science IPA with Riwaka & Citra
• Green Kush West Coast IPA with Chinook, Strata & Citra Cryo

🍊 Local Haze
• Aslin Johann Buys a Broat Citra DIPA with Pineapple & Mandarin Orange
• Aslin Master of Oranges DIPA with Galaxy, Mosaic, Citra & Amarillo
• Solace Thunder Buddy IPA with Mandarina Bavaria & Citra

🌞 The Veil getting you ready for the heat
• Schroderhaust Open Fermented Weissebier
• Dusty Roadz Hazy Pale Ale with Citra, Citra Cryo, and Nelson Sauvin
• Thrown Thrown DDH West Coast IPA with Citra, Simcoe, Amarillo, Columbus & Simcoe Hyperboost

🙌 12percent imports FTW
• Fat Orange Cat Crazy Cat Lady DDH DIPA with Krush, Idaho 7 & Citra
• Hoof Hearted Wet My Hair For The Battle Cryo Wet-Hopped DIPA with Citra, Sabro & Simcoe
• Off Color Beer For Golf Witbier with Lemon & Black Tea

🍺 Easy drinkers
• Maine Another One IPA, Lunch IPA & Peeper Pale Ale
• Einbecker Unfiltered Brauherren Keller Pils

Troegs special release time!

• Nimble Giant West Coast DIPA

🌟 Welcome to The Brew Shop, Moia Sparkling Water! Locally crafted by brother + sister duo, Farris and Raihan, Moia is the first ever middle eastern sparkling water brand bringing you the culture 🌙 vibes ✨ of Marrakech and Kabul and everything else in between, all conveniently in sparkling water form!

🌟 Find Moia cold as single cans in our mini-fridge on the front counter and in 12-packs ready to share!

Adroit Theory dropping in tomorrow
• Necrosynth Quadruple Hazy IPA with Citra, El Dorado, Mosaic & Amarillo
• Death of Civilization in Slow Motion I. Hazy IPA with Citra, Mosaic & El Dorado
• Death.Net West Coast IPA with Citra, Strata & Chinook
• Midnight Special Russian Imperial Stout

Halfway Crooks Var Czech Pilsner

Brainstorm Let Him Cook Hazy IPA

Schilling Jelen Czech Dark Lager

New wines in the door!

Fantini Lava Campania Falanghina:Falanghina is one of Italy's great comeback stories — nearly lost to extinction in the mid-20th century when growers ripped out their vines in favor of more fashionable varieties. We're glad Falanghina made a comeback! Fantini Lava Falanghina comes from Campania's volcanic soils, shaped by ancient eruptions and rich in ash, pumice, and mineral deposits — all of which give the wine its signature sense of place. Think citrus blossom, ripe pear and a whisper of volcanic minerality that ties it all together.

Field Recordings Shucks: We are just a little bit obsessed with Andrew Jones' new release and ultimate "oyster wine," Shucks. Crisp with saline-laced texture and notes of wet stone, zippy green apple and lemon-infused acidity. With 100% ocean vibes, this bottle is indeed built for pairing with shellfish.

SoHu Orange Wine (returns!): We've been hotly anticipating the return of this orange wine from SoHu. Expectations were exceeded with lively, fresh peach and apricot paired with orange blossom. Easily quaffable and loveable. Hand Harvested, Native Yeast

We're rolling into one of the biggest weekends of the year where we have the delightful collision of Taylor Swift's wedding and America's 250th birthday. I personally can't think of a better reason to raise a glass. My pick for this celebratory, hot as hell weekend is Born Rambla Rose.

It's probably no surprise that we love supporting other women-owned businesses. Born Rose comes to us from a group of women from Barcelona who were so passionate about rosé that they pooled their energy and savings and built a winery that exclusively produces Rose. Among the all pink line-up of still and sparkling Roses, we fell for the serious, but crush worthy Rambla Rose which is of course named for Barcelona's most famous boulevard — a living, breathing, permanently sun-drenched promenade where the city does its best showing off.

Born Rambla Rose is made with 60% Garnacha and 40% Tempranillo, grown organically and fermented in stainless steel tanks to preserve the grapes' natural freshness and aroma. Grenache brings the sun-drenched Mediterranean fruit and floral aromatics; Tempranillo adds structure and a grounding earthiness. Pouring ballet pink with notes of peach, strawberry, citrus and a dry, mineral-tinged finish. And like all of Born Rosé's wines, Rambla Rose is 100% organic and clocks in with a low alcohol level - so go ahead and pour yourself a second glass. As an added bonus, Rambla Rose has a patio friendly screw cap which is perfect for this long holiday weekend of celebration.

This week’s writeup is brought to you by Beth.

July Tasting Events coming your way!

Thanks for your business! See you soon, Beth, Julie & Aidan and the entire Brew Crew
